title: Instrument for minimal invasive surgical operations, comprising a rod or catheter and a tip mounted on the rod or catheter with an elastically deformable element
abstract: Instrument for minimal invasive surgical operations, comprising a rod or catheter and a tip mounted on the rod or catheter with an elastically deformable element, wherein the elastically deformable element is arranged to control a con- tact force applied with the tip on a tissue that is subjected to the surgical operations, and wherein the elastically deformable element is designed to provide a substantially constant force to the tip when a distance between the tip and the rod or catheter that supports the tip is varied within a pre- defined range.
claims:
Claims 1. Instrument (1) for minimal invasive surgical operations, comprising a rod or catheter (2) and a tip (3) mounted on the rod or catheter (2) with an elastically deformable ele Â¬ ment (4), wherein the elastically deformable element (4) is arranged to apply during use a contact force with the tip (3) on a tissue that is subjected to the surgical operations, characterized in that the elastically deformable element (4) is designed to provide a substantially constant force to the tip (3} when a distance between the tip (3) and the rod or catheter (2) that supports the tip (3) is varied within a pre Â¬ defined range. 2. Instrument according to claim 1, characterized in that the deformable element (4) provides the tip (3) with zero-stiffness or substantially zero-stiffness when moved in a range in a longitudinal direction of the rod or catheter (2) . 3. Instrument according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the deformable element (4) autonomously resists bending in a direction transverse to the longitudinal direction of the rod or catheter (2). 4. Instrument according to any one of claims 1 - 3, characterized in that the deformable element (4) has a monolithic structure. 5. Instrument according to any one of claims 1 - 4, or according to the preamble of claim 1, characterized in that the elastically deformable element (4) is an endless tape loop spring (5) with predefined parameters relating to at least its material, combination of materials, or positions in the tape loop spring where such material is missing, the tape loop spring width (6), the tape loop spring material thickness, and the tape loop spring's transverse curvature (7), and wherein the tape loop spring (5) is embodied with two elongated parts {8, 9) in or parallel to a longitudinal direction (10) of the rod or catheter (2), which elongated parts (8, 9) convert into each other by bends (11, 12) at opposite sides of the elongat- ed parts (8, 9) , and wherein each bend (11, 12) connects the elongated parts (8, 9) to each other, and wherein at least one of the said parameters of the endless tape loop spring (5) is 

varied along the circumference of the tape loop spring (5) , and wherein variation of the distance between the tip (3) and the rod or catheter (2) causes a circumferential round going motion of the endless tape loop spring (5) which causes that the bends (11, 12) travel along the circumference of the end Â¬ less loop tape spring (5) while maintaining their position in or parallel to the longitudinal direction (10) of the rod or catheter (2) . 6. Instrument according to claim 5, characterized in that the width (6) of the tape loop spring (5) is varied in the circumferential direction of the tape loop spring (5) . 7. Instrument according to claim 6, characterized in that the tape loop spring (5) has an unloaded position wherein respective parts of the tape loop spring (5) occupy a prefer- ential position with respect to each other, and wherein at or near the location of the bends (11, 12) the width (6) of the tape loop spring (5) is smaller than at the two elongated parts (8, 9) between the bends (11, 12). 8. Instrument according to any one of claims 5 - 7, characterized in that the endless tape loop spring (5) has a width with tapered portions. 9. Instrument according to claim 8, characterized in that the tapered portions extend along the length of the elongated parts (8, 9) of the endless tape loop spring (5). 10. Instrument according to claims 8 or 9, characterized in that the tapered portions have their smallest width (6) at or near the bends (11, 12) when the tape loop spring (5) is in the unloaded position. 11. Instrument according to any one of claims 8 - 10, characterized in that the tapered portions are provided with an imaginary top angle or subtended angle in the range of 95 - 135Â° . 12. Instrument according to any one of the previous claims 5 - 11, characterized in that in a transverse direction of the endless tape loop spring (5) , the spring has a curvature (7) with a predefined radius of curvature. 13. Instrument according to claim 12, characterized in that the predefined radius of curvature (7) is preferably 

selected at a value corresponding to the rod or catheter (2) diameter . 14. Instrument according to any one of the previous claims 1 - 13, characterized in that the instrument (1) is fo tissue ablation, particularly in the heart.
